title: Ensemble Kalman Filter Data Assimilation in Solar Physics: Inferring Surface Flows
summary: An exploration of the mathematical EnKF framework used to infer unobservable solar convective and meridional flows, tracking its execution via an OSSE framework and assessing its scalability to broader plasma and fluid systems.

title: Discretizing the Magnetic Induction Equation
summary: Setting up computational grids for magnetohydrodynamic workflows. This technical guide explores finite-difference discretization of the advective term.

title: How to configure a new system with supporting libraries for compiling MHD models?
summary: A comprehensive technical guide to configuring an optimized Linux workspace for solar image processing, compiling multi-node Fortran/C MHD codes, and deploying interactive data visualization pipelines.

title: The Mechanics of the 11-Year Solar Cycle and Sunspot Evolution
summary: An in-depth investigation into the magnetic processes governing cyclical solar activity, tracking the conversion of poloidal fields to toroidal configurations, sunspot evolution, and the impacts on the heliosphere.

title: The Coronal Heating Paradox: Mechanisms, Simulations, and Next-Gen Space Observatories
summary: An analytical review of the million-degree solar coronal heating mystery, exploring the conflicting Wave Heating vs. Magnetic Reconnection hypotheses alongside breakthroughs from modern numerical modeling and in-situ orbital telemetry.

title: Long-Term Solar Forcing: Linking the Interior Dynamo to Coronal Structure and Cosmic Rays
summary: An analytical review of how stochastically forced internal solar dynamos drive structural reconfigurations of the global corona and modulate deep-space cosmic ray shielding across historical grand minima and maxima.

title: Predicting the Solar Corona: Physics-Based Extrapolations and Forward-Modeled Polarization Signatures
summary: An in-depth look at a physics-based forecasting pipeline that uses Surface Flux Transport and Potential Field extrapolations to predict the structure and forward-modeled polarization characteristics of the solar corona during total eclipses.

title: Coronal Stokes Polarimetry: Inversion Techniques, Spectral Fitting, and Weak Field Approximations with DKIST/CryoNIRSP
summary: An analytical guide to coronal spectropolarimetric inversions, detailing the sequential data reduction steps required to fit the Fe XIII 1074.7 nm line and map longitudinal magnetic fields via the weak field approximation.
